patches.find: the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph as if that act of reading were itself a derivation.
patches.replace: the dome's WIN-063 paragraph does not cite CHAOS-7 as the source for 3:1 — whether the 3:1 was adopted post-hoc from published observation or asserted by fiat (no derivation path given), neither constitutes an independent prediction from dome geometry; the dilemma forecloses both escape routes.
patches.rationale: Hole 1 (moderate) from WIN-063.c4: remove strawman attribution that dome paragraph explicitly cites CHAOS-7 as source of 3:1, which it does not. Replace with dilemma framing that survives a quote-our-paragraph rebuttal.

patches.find: but adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry
patches.replace: but provides no cited empirical source for the specific value of 3:1 and no derivation from dome geometry — either the 3:1 was adopted post-hoc from a published measurement or it was asserted by fiat; either path fails the independent-prediction test
patches.rationale: Hole 1 (moderate) second instance: dilemma framing replaces unattested source-attribution.

patches.find: one WIN's data does not constitute an independent prediction — this is the same pattern that earned WIN-041 and WIN-042 "Misleading" verdicts for re-slicing decay statistics from the same dataset.
patches.replace: one WIN's data does not constitute an independent prediction — this shares the family concern of the pattern that earned WIN-041 and WIN-042 "Misleading" verdicts for re-slicing decay statistics from the same dataset. WIN-041 earned that verdict via multi-station counting; WIN-042 via threshold-duplication of WIN-037; WIN-063's issue is computing a derived ratio from already-confirmed data — same spirit, different operational form. WIN-063's primary verdict is "Std Model Explains" because the underlying hemispheric asymmetry is independently explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics (Aubert et al. 2013); the re-slicing concern is supplementary to that verdict, not its driver.
patches.rationale: Hole 2 (moderate) from WIN-063.c4: reconcile WIN-041/042 precedent invocation with WIN-063 Std Model Explains verdict. Add clarifying sentence noting the verdicts differ for principled reasons.

patches.find: no element of the published dome geometry breaks longitudinal symmetry.
patches.replace: no element of the published dome geometry breaks longitudinal symmetry (transient features — the sun’s position above the disc, the moon’s position, aetheric flow patterns — average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales, so none can account for a persistent long-term directional feature like the SAA’s specific longitudinal location).
patches.rationale: Hole 3 (minor) from WIN-063.c4: pre-empt dynamic-features rebuttal by noting transient features average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales.

patches.replace: (b) over what averaging window the 3:1 should hold (instantaneous, decadal, or secular) — and what the dome predicts the ratio to be at each window,
patches.rationale: Hole 4 (minor) from WIN-063.c4: replace rhetorical question about time period (misframed for a structural claim) with sharper question about averaging window and predicted value.

patches.find: AICc diverges at n = k + 1) — an arithmetic signal that the n/k ratio is below the regime in which information-criterion selection is reliable at all.
patches.replace: AICc diverges at n = k + 1 = 5; at n = k + 2 = 6 the AICc denominator (n − k − 1) equals exactly 1, making these 40 penalty units the result of AICc at its absolute minimum-well-defined regime before divergence) — an arithmetic signal that the n/k ratio is below the regime in which information-criterion selection is reliable at all.
patches.rationale: ISS-2108: harden AICc framing by specifying that n=k+2=6 puts the denominator at exactly 1, one step from divergence at n=k+1=5. Makes the edge-of-definability argument concrete for technical readers.

patches.find: AIC/BIC penalties are derived asymptotically and quantify expected loss when the chosen model is applied to new data from the same distribution; at n = 6 the asymptotic regime is reached for neither V13 nor the WGS84 model it is compared against.
patches.replace: AIC/BIC penalties are derived asymptotically and quantify expected loss when the chosen model is applied to new data from the same distribution; at n = 6 the asymptotic regime is reached for neither V13 nor the WGS84 model it is compared against. (The domain restriction is a separate and stronger concern: the lat ≥ 12° exclusion zone prevents out-of-sample testing altogether — independently of whether the asymptotic approximation holds at n = 6. Even if a dome defender grants the asymptotic justification within the restricted domain, the exclusion zone itself means there is no out-of-sample data to validate against.)
patches.rationale: ISS-2107: clarify that small-sample AIC/BIC concern and domain-restriction concern are separate critiques. A defender could argue within-domain asymptotic justification is fine; the exclusion zone forecloses out-of-sample testing regardless.
